titleStmt.title: A transcription of Romans in 61
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Epistle to the Romans
Romans

respStmt.name: The International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: This transcription was made as part of the CATENA project, which has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no. 770816).

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

editionStmt.edition: Version 1.1, created on 17.03.2022, archived on the University of Birmingham eData repository on 13.5.2024.

publisher.name: The International Greek New Testament Project (IGNTP)

availability.p: Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) Available for re-use under a creative commons license provided attribution is made to the original creators.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription was produced using the online transcription editor developed by the Center for Digital Humanities at the University of Trier as part of the Workspace for Collaborative Editing Project between 2010 and 2013 and made available for use by the Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing and the University of Birmingham.
The XML specifications used are version 1.6 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

ab.note: An enlarged initial π in παυλος was probably intended but not completed. The small guide letter can still be seen.
An enlarged initial δ in διο was probably intended but not completed. The small guide letter can still be seen.
An enlarged initial τ in τι was probably intended but not completed. The small guide letter can still be seen.
An enlarged initial τ in τι was probably intended but not completed. The small guide letter can still be seen.
An enlarged initial δ in δικαιωθεντες was probably intended but not completed. The small guide letter can still be seen.
An enlarged letter τ to make τι was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
A space was left for an enlarged initial η which was not written. The small guide letter can still be seen.
An enlarged letter ο to make ουδεν was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter α to make αληθειαν was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter α to make αδελφοι was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter λ to make λεγω was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter π to make παρακαλω was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter π to make πασα was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter τ to make τον was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged letter ο to make οφειλομεν was probably intended but not written. The small guide letter can still be seen in the margin.
An enlarged intial letter σ to make συνιστημι was probably intended but not completed. The small guide letter cannot be seen in this instance.
